questions.questionText: Which of the following letters has exactly two lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter 'H' has two lines of symmetry: one horizontal and one vertical. 'A' has one, 'S' and 'Z' have rotational symmetry but no lines of symmetry.

questions.questionText: A regular hexagon has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry, passing through opposite vertices and midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.questionText: What is the order of rotational symmetry of a square?
questions.explanation: A square can be rotated by 90°, 180°, 270°, and 360° to look identical. This means it has rotational symmetry of order 4.

questions.questionText: Which of these shapes has point symmetry but no lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A parallelogram has rotational symmetry of order 2 (point symmetry) about its centre, but generally has no lines of symmetry unless it is a rhombus or a rectangle.

questions.questionText: A shape has rotational symmetry of order 3. How many degrees must it be rotated to map onto itself for the first time?
questions.explanation: The angle of rotation for a shape with order of rotational symmetry 'n' is 360°/n. For $n = 3$, the angle is 360°/3 = 120°.

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about a kite is true?
options: It has two lines of symmetry.
It has rotational symmetry of order 2.
It has exactly one line of symmetry.
questions.explanation: A kite has exactly one line of symmetry, which is the diagonal between the vertices where the unequal sides meet.

questions.questionText: A shape is reflected in the x-axis. Which coordinate changes sign?
questions.explanation: When a point (x, y) is reflected in the x-axis, its image is (x, -y). The y-coordinate changes sign, while the x-coordinate remains the same.

questions.questionText: A design has an order of rotational symmetry of 5. How many times does it map onto itself during a full turn (excluding the 360° point)?
questions.explanation: An order of rotational symmetry of 5 means the design maps onto itself 5 times in a full turn (360°). If we exclude the 360° point (which is the starting position), it maps onto itself 5 - 1 = 4 times.

questions.questionText: Which of these numbers has no lines of symmetry and no rotational symmetry (order > 1)?
questions.explanation: The number '2' has no lines of symmetry and no rotational symmetry of order greater than 1. '0' has infinite symmetry, '1' has one line, '8' has two lines and order 2 rotational symmetry.

questions.questionText: A square is rotated 180° about its centre. What is the effect on its position?
options: It maps onto its original position.
It is reflected in the x-axis.
It moves to a new quadrant.
It is reflected in a diagonal.
questions.explanation: A square has rotational symmetry of order 4. Rotating it by 180° (which is 360°/2) will make it look identical to its original position, hence it maps onto itself.

questions.questionText: How many lines of symmetry does a semicircle have?
questions.explanation: A semicircle has exactly one line of symmetry, which passes through its centre and is perpendicular to its diameter.

questions.questionText: A regular pentagon has an order of rotational symmetry of:
questions.explanation: A regular pentagon has 5 equal sides and 5 equal angles. It can be rotated 5 times by 72° each time to map onto itself. Therefore, its order of rotational symmetry is 5.
